subject: [PATCH v2 15/21] nfsd: validate sockaddr length per family in listener_set

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS daemon's netlink listener set functionality, where a CAP_NET_ADMIN caller could send an invalid sockaddr structure that would cause out-of-bounds reads across multiple consumers. The patch tightens the policy for the NFSD_A_SOCK_ADDR attribute to require at least 16 bytes and adds per-family length validation in both nlmsg_for_each_attr_type loops. This ensures that only valid sockaddr structures are processed, preventing potential security vulnerabilities.
